comment_text.sn35.31:1.1: “Conducive to” (sappāya) is a synonym of “fit for” (sāruppa) in the previous discourse.
comment_text.sn35.31:1.8: Bhava means “existence” or “being”, not “becoming”. Those who are “attached to being” are not attached to “becoming” something else; that would be vibhavataṇhā, “craving for nonexistence”. They are are attached to a state of eternal existence, to continuing to exist in another life (bhavataṇhā). They conceive this to be an eternal self, but their conceiving is ultimately derived from sense experience, which is always changing.

root_text.sn35.31:1.3: Katamā ca sā, bhikkhave, sabbamaññitasamugghātasappāyā paṭipadā?
root_text.sn35.31:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cakkhuṁ na maññati, cakkhusmiṁ na maññati, cakkhuto na maññati, cakkhuṁ meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.31:1.6: cakkhuviññāṇaṁ na maññati, cakkhusamphassaṁ na maññati, yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi na maññati, tasmimpi na maññati, tatopi na maññati, taṁ meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.31:1.7: Yañhi, bhikkhave, maññati, yasmiṁ maññati, yato maññati, yaṁ meti maññati, tato taṁ hoti aññathā.
root_text.sn35.31:1.9: jivhaṁ na maññati, jivhāya na maññati, jivhāto na maññati, jivhā meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.31:1.11: jivhāviññāṇaṁ na maññati, jivhāsamphassaṁ na maññati.
root_text.sn35.31:1.12: Yampidaṁ jivhāsamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi na maññati, tasmimpi na maññati, tatopi na maññati, taṁ meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.31:1.13: Yañhi, bhikkhave, maññati, yasmiṁ maññati, yato maññati, yaṁ meti maññati, tato taṁ hoti aññathā.
root_text.sn35.31:1.15: manaṁ na maññati, manasmiṁ na maññati, manato na maññati, mano meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.31:1.17: manoviññāṇaṁ na maññati, manosamphassaṁ na maññati.
root_text.sn35.31:1.18: Yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi na maññati, tasmimpi na maññati, tatopi na maññati, taṁ meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.31:1.19: Yañhi, bhikkhave, maññati, yasmiṁ maññati, yato maññati, yaṁ meti maññati, tato taṁ hoti aññathā.
root_text.sn35.31:1.21: Yāvatā, bhikkhave, khandhadhātuāyatanaṁ tampi na maññati, tasmimpi na maññati, tatopi na maññati, taṁ meti na maññati.
root_text.sn35.31:1.22: So evaṁ amaññamāno na ca kiñci loke upādiyati.
root_text.sn35.31:1.23: Anupādiyaṁ na paritassati. Aparitassaṁ paccattaññeva parinibbāyati.
root_text.sn35.31:1.24: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn35.31:1.25: Ayaṁ kho sā, bhikkhave, sabbamaññitasamugghātasappāyā paṭipadā”ti.

translation_text.sn35.31:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you the practice that’s conducive to uprooting all conceiving.
translation_text.sn35.31:1.3: And what is the practice that’s conducive to uprooting all conceiving?
translation_text.sn35.31:1.4: It’s when a mendicant does not conceive anything to be the eye, does not conceive it in the eye, does not conceive it as the eye, and does not conceive that ‘the eye is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.31:1.5: They don’t conceive anything to be sights …
translation_text.sn35.31:1.6: eye consciousness … eye contact. And they don’t conceive anything to be the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact. They don’t conceive it in that, they don’t conceive it as that, and they don’t conceive that ‘that is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.31:1.7: For whatever you conceive it to be, whatever you conceive it in, whatever you conceive it as, and whatever you conceive to be ‘mine’: that becomes something else.
translation_text.sn35.31:1.8: The world is attached to being, taking pleasure only in being, yet it becomes something else.
translation_text.sn35.31:1.9: They don’t conceive anything to be the ear … nose … tongue …
translation_text.sn35.31:1.18: They don’t conceive anything to be the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact. They don’t conceive it in that, they don’t conceive it as that, and they don’t conceive that ‘that is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.31:1.19: For whatever you conceive it to be, whatever you conceive it in, whatever you conceive it as, and whatever you conceive to be ‘mine’: that becomes something else.
translation_text.sn35.31:1.20: The world is attached to being, taking pleasure only in being, yet it becomes something else.
translation_text.sn35.31:1.21: As far as the aggregates, elements, and sense fields extend, they don’t conceive anything to be that, they don’t conceive it in that, they don’t conceive it as that, and they don’t conceive that ‘that is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.31:1.22: Not conceiving, they don’t grasp at anything in the world.
translation_text.sn35.31:1.23: Not grasping, they’re not anxious. Not being anxious, they personally become extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.31:1.24: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn35.31:1.25: This is the practice that’s conducive to uprooting all conceiving.”
